title: SSH Tunneling & Port Forwarding
description: Using SSH to create encrypted tunnels that forward TCP connections through a secure channel.
fullExplanation: SSH tunneling encapsulates other protocols inside an SSH connection, providing encryption and bypassing firewalls. Local forwarding makes a remote service accessible locally. Remote forwarding exposes a local service to the remote server. Dynamic forwarding creates a SOCKS proxy. Jump hosts (ProxyJump) chain SSH connections through bastion hosts. SSH tunneling is used for secure database access, bypassing firewalls, and accessing internal services.

types.description: Forward local port 8080 to internal-db:5432 through bastion. Connect to localhost:8080 to reach the database.
types.useCase: Access internal database through bastion host

types.description: Forward public-server:8080 to localhost:3000. Expose local dev server to internet.
types.useCase: Expose local service for demos or webhooks

types.description: Create SOCKS5 proxy on local port 1080. All traffic routed through SSH server.
types.useCase: Browse internet through SSH server, bypass censorship

types.description: Connect to internal-server through bastion in one command. Replaces old ProxyCommand.
types.useCase: Access servers only reachable through bastion host

keyFacts: SSH tunnels encrypt all forwarded traffic — even non-encrypted protocols become secure
GatewayPorts: allow remote forwarding to bind on all interfaces (not just localhost)
AllowTcpForwarding no: disable tunneling in sshd_config for restricted servers
SSH tunneling can bypass firewalls — security teams monitor for unauthorized tunnels
autossh: automatically restart SSH tunnels if they drop
~/.ssh/config: define tunnel shortcuts to avoid long command lines
VPN vs SSH tunnel: VPN routes all traffic, SSH tunnel routes specific ports
